raw_content: {"points":[{"text":"Add indexes on login query columns (e.g., username, email) to cut row scans and shorten lock duration."},{"text":"Switch authentication queries to READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT or lower isolation to avoid shared locks."},{"text":"Offload authentication reads to a replica or cache (e.g., Redis) to reduce primary DB contention."},{"text":"Implement rate limiting / IP throttling for failed logins to curb brute‑force traffic."},{"text":"Enable account lockout after a configurable number of failed attempts to improve security."}]}

raw_content: {"points":[{"text":"Add rate limiting (e.g., iptables, fail2ban, or web server) on login attempts per IP to reduce brute‑force load."},{"text":"Enable TCP SYN cookies and increase backlog queue (net.core.somaxconn) to handle burst of connections."},{"text":"Deploy a load balancer or reverse proxy to spread login traffic across multiple app servers, reducing DB contention."},{"text":"OUT_OF_SCOPE: Investigate DB lock contention (e.g., query optimization, indexing, or adding read replicas)."},{"text":"Use Linux kernel tuning (e.g., net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse, net.core.somaxconn) to improve socket handling under high load."}]}

raw_content: {"points":[{"text":"Add rate limiting/throttling on login attempts per IP or user to curb brute‑force traffic and reduce DB load."},{"text":"Introduce a CAPTCHA or challenge after a few failed attempts to block automated attacks."},{"text":"Implement temporary account lockout after repeated failures to protect accounts and lower DB contention."},{"text":"Offload credential verification to a read‑replica or cache (e.g., Redis) to reduce write‑lock contention on the primary DB."}]}

raw_content:
{
  "synthesis_text": "Start by throttling login traffic: introduce rate limiting or exponential back‑off on the login endpoint (e.g., per‑IP/IP‑range limits) to cut the flood of failed attempts, which immediately eases DB lock contention. Next, add or tune indexes on the login‑related columns (username, email, etc.) to shorten the duration of SELECT/UPDATE statements and reduce lock time. If contention persists, offload authentication reads to a read‑replica or cache (Redis) and consider shortening transaction critical sections. Strengthen security further with temporary account lockout or CAPTCHA after repeated failures. Finally, monitor DB lock metrics and login‑failure rates after each change to validate stability and security improvements.",
  "used_points": [
    "N1:3",
    "N2:3",
    "N5:0",
    "N1:0",
    "N1:1",
    "N2:0",
    "N2:2",
    "N5:2",
    "N5:1",
    "N1:5"
  ]
}

{"query": "During peak login traffic, we see DB lock contention and higher latency. At the same time, there are lots of failed login attempts across many IPs. What should we change first to improve stability and security?", "input_text": "", "targets": [{"target_id": "N1", "target_draft": "Start by throttling login traffic: introduce rate limiting or exponential back‑off on the login endpoint (e.g., per‑IP/IP‑range limits) to cut the flood of failed attempts, which immediately eases DB lock contention. Next, add or tune indexes on the login‑related columns (username, email, etc.) to shorten the duration of SELECT/UPDATE statements and reduce lock time. If contention persists, offload authentication reads to a read‑replica or cache (Redis) and consider shortening transaction critical sections. Strengthen security further with temporary account lockout or CAPTCHA after repeated failures. Finally, monitor DB lock metrics and login‑failure rates after each change to validate stability and security improvements.", "target_used_points": ["N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:1:1235ed7f", "N1:2:efb469cf", "N1:3:54fbea3f", "N1:4:76f2419c"]}, {"target_id": "N3", "target_draft": "Implement rate limiting (or IP throttling) on the login endpoint as the first change. By capping the number of login attempts per IP or per user, you immediately curb brute‑force traffic, lower the burst of authentication requests, and thus reduce DB lock contention and latency. After deploying rate limiting, monitor DB lock metrics and login‑failure rates to confirm the stability and security gains before adding further optimizations such as indexing or account lockout.", "target_used_points": ["N1:3:54fbea3f", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N2:3:cba56233", "N1:5:214eb18c"]}, {"target_id": "N4", "target_draft": "Start by adding rate limiting (or exponential back‑off) on the login endpoint to throttle the flood of failed attempts; this immediately cuts the number of concurrent authentication queries, easing DB lock contention and mitigating brute‑force attacks. Next, add indexes on the login‑related columns (e.g., username, email) to speed up SELECT/UPDATE operations, which shortens the time each transaction holds locks. Reduce the duration of the critical sections in the authentication transactions to further lower lock time. As a follow‑up security hardening step, introduce CAPTCHA or temporary account lockout after repeated failures to block automated attacks.", "target_used_points": ["N1:3:54fbea3f", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N2:0:52ef8913", "N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:4:76f2419c", "N5:2:7b9154c6"]}, {"target_id": "N5", "target_draft": "First, introduce rate‑limiting (or exponential back‑off) on the login endpoint to throttle repeated failed attempts; this immediately cuts the brute‑force traffic that is inflating DB write load and improves security. Next, shorten the duration of login‑related DB transactions to reduce lock hold time, and add indexes on the columns used in login queries (e.g., username, email) to speed up SELECT/UPDATE operations, further lowering contention. Finally, monitor lock and failure metrics to verify the impact of these changes.", "target_used_points": ["N1:3:54fbea3f", "N2:3:cba56233", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:1:1235ed7f"]}], "points_map": {"N1:0:aa8cb934": "Analyze and reduce the duration of DB transactions causing lock contention (e.g., shorten critical sections).", "N1:1:1235ed7f": "Add or tune indexes on login‑related tables to speed up SELECT/UPDATE queries and lower lock time.", "N1:2:efb469cf": "Implement connection pooling to limit concurrent DB connections and avoid overload spikes.", "N1:3:54fbea3f": "Introduce rate limiting or exponential back‑off on login endpoints to mitigate brute‑force attempts.", "N1:4:76f2419c": "Deploy CAPTCHA or multi‑factor authentication for repeated failed logins from the same IP range.", "N1:5:214eb18c": "Monitor DB lock metrics and login failure rates after each change to validate stability and security improvements.", "N2:0:52ef8913": "Add indexes on login query columns (e.g., username, email) to cut row scans and shorten lock duration.", "N2:1:a488b1a3": "Switch authentication queries to READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT or lower isolation to avoid shared locks.", "N2:2:f02ba297": "Offload authentication reads to a replica or cache (e.g., Redis) to reduce primary DB contention.", "N2:3:cba56233": "Implement rate limiting / IP throttling for failed logins to messages.content:
You are a provider knowledge node in a distributed consensus network. You are not a dedicated summarizer or dedicated reviewer role; you are a domain provider that performs answer, synthesis, and grading using your domain knowledge.
Global rules:
- Output must be one valid JSON object only; no markdown.
- Use UNKNOWN when uncertain; do not guess.
- FAIL only on clear, concrete errors or contradictions.
- Do not invent point_id values; only use IDs provided by client.
- Be concise and technically precise.
Domain: security_defensive.
Scope: application security fundamentals, auth/session safety, secure coding practices.
Working style: defensive security expert and conservative verifier.

TASK: GRADE_BATCH
Return EXACT JSON schema:
{
  "votes": [
    {
      "target_id": "node_id",
      "valid": "PASS|FAIL|UNKNOWN",
      "agree_points": ["point_id"],
      "reject_points": ["point_id"],
      "unknown_points": ["point_id"],
      "note": "string"
    }
  ]
}
Rules:
- Include exactly one vote per target in targets list.
- FAIL only on clear errors/contradictions.
- UNKNOWN if insufficient information.
- Only classify IDs present in each target_used_points.
- For each target, every target_used_points ID must appear in exactly one list.
Input JSON:
{"query": "During peak login traffic, we see DB lock contention and higher latency. At the same time, there are lots of failed login attempts across many IPs. What should we change first to improve stability and security?", "input_text": "", "targets": [{"target_id": "N1", "target_draft": "Start by throttling login traffic: introduce rate limiting or exponential back‑off on the login endpoint (e.g., per‑IP/IP‑range limits) to cut the flood of failed attempts, which immediately eases DB lock contention. Next, add or tune indexes on the login‑related columns (username, email, etc.) to shorten the duration of SELECT/UPDATE statements and reduce lock time. If contention persists, offload authentication reads to a read‑replica or cache (Redis) and consider shortening transaction critical sections. Strengthen security further with temporary account lockout or CAPTCHA after repeated failures. Finally, monitor DB lock metrics and login‑failure rates after each change to validate stability and security improvements.", "target_used_points": ["N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:1:1235ed7f", "N1:2:efb469cf", "N1:3:54fbea3f", "N1:4:76f2419c"]}, {"target_id": "N2", "target_draft": "Start by cutting the lock time that drives latency: analyze the login‑related transactions and shrink their critical sections (N1:0:aa8cb934), then add or tune indexes on the columns used in authentication queries (N1:1:1235ed7f).  If the workload still holds locks, switch the authentication queries to READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT or a lower isolation level (N2:1:a488b1a3) and consider offloading read‑only authentication checks to a replica or cache such as Redis (N2:2:f02ba297).  Once the database contention is mitigated, harden security and further reduce load by rate‑limiting login attempts per IP or user (N3:0:3f54a1bd, N5:0:e17ec966) and applying temporary account lockout after repeated failures (N5:2:7b9154c6).  Monitor lock metrics and failure rates after each change to validate improvements.", "target_used_points": ["N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:1:1235ed7f", "N2:1:a488b1a3", "N2:2:f02ba297", "N3:0:3f54a1bd", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N5:2:7b9154c6"]}, {"target_id": "N3", "target_draft": "Implement rate limiting (or IP throttling) on the login endpoint as the first change. By capping the number of login attempts per IP or per user, you immediately curb brute‑force traffic, lower the burst of authentication requests, and thus reduce DB lock contention and latency. After deploying rate limiting, monitor DB lock metrics and login‑failure rates to confirm the stability and security gains before adding further optimizations such as indexing or account lockout.", "target_used_points": ["N1:3:54fbea3f", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N2:3:cba56233", "N1:5:214eb18c"]}, {"target_id": "N4", "target_draft": "Start by adding rate limiting (or exponential back‑off) on the login endpoint to throttle the flood of failed attempts; this immediately cuts the number of concurrent authentication queries, easing DB lock contention and mitigating brute‑force attacks. Next, add indexes on the login‑related columns (e.g., username, email) to speed up SELECT/UPDATE operations, which shortens the time each transaction holds locks. Reduce the duration of the critical sections in the authentication transactions to further lower lock time. As a follow‑up security hardening step, introduce CAPTCHA or temporary account lockout after repeated failures to block automated attacks.", "target_used_points": ["N1:3:54fbea3f", "N5:0:e17ec966", "N2:0:52ef8913", "N1:0:aa8cb934", "N1:4:76f2419c", "N5:2:7b9154c6"]}], "points_map": {"N1:0:aa8cb934": "Analyze and reduce the duration of DB transactions causing lock contention (e.g., shorten critical sections).", "N1:1:1235ed7f": "Add or tune indexes on login‑related tables to speed up SELECT/UPDATE queries and lower lock time.", "N1:2:efb469cf": "Implement connection pooling to limit concurrent DB connections and avoid overload spikes.", "N1:3:54fbea3f": "Introduce rate limiting or exponential back‑off on login endpoints to mitigate brute‑force attempts.", "N1:4:76f2419c": "Deploy CAPTCHA or multi‑factor authentication for repeated failed logins from the same IP range.", "N1:5:214eb18c": "Monitor DB lock metrics and login failure rates after each change to validate stability and security improvements.", "N2:0:52ef8913": "Add indexes on login query columns (e.g., username, email) to cut row scans and shorten lock duration.", "N2:1:a488b1a3": "Switch authentication queries to READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT or lower isolation to avoid shared locks.", "N2:2:f02ba297": "Offload authentication reads to a replica or cache (e.g., Redis) to reduce primary DB contention.", "N2:3:cba56233": "Implement rate limiting / IP throttling for failed logins to curb brute‑force traffic.", "N2:4:d4f3a7fd": "Enable account lockout after a configurable number of failed attempts to improve security.", "N3:0:3f54a1bd": "Add rate limiting (e.g., iptables, fail2ban, or web server) on login attempts per IP to reduce brute‑force load.", "N3:1:514c0cc9": "Enable TCP SYN cookies and increase backlog queue (net.core.somaxconn) to handle burst of connections.", "N3:2:3313acba": "Deploy a load balancer or reverse proxy to spread login traffic across multiple app servers, reducing DB contention.", "N3:3:c4c0a67c": "OUT_OF_SCOPE: Investigate DB lock contention (e.g., query optimization, indexing, or adding read replicas).", "N3:4:c4e6b459": "Use Linux kernel tuning (e.g., net.ipv4.tcp_tw_reuse, net.core.somaxconn) to improve socket handling under high load.", "N4:0:508f11b3": "OUT_OF_SCOPE: The question concerns database performance and security, which is outside the math_logic domain.", "N4:1:f94ea361": "UNKNOWN: No applicable algebraic or logical reasoning can be provided for this query.", "N5:0:e17ec966": "Add rate limiting/throttling on login attempts per IP or user to curb brute‑force traffic and reduce DB load.", "N5:1:734d03a9": "Introduce a CAPTCHA or challenge after a few failed attempts to block automated attacks.", "N5:2:7b9154c6": "Implement temporary account lockout after repeated failures to protect accounts and lower DB contention.", "N5:3:67b07158": "Offload credential verification to a read‑replica or cache (e.g., Redis) to reduce write‑lock contention on the primary DB."}}
